title: PyTorch and OneFlow Extension Op Boundary

problem.summary: Define a framework extension boundary for a rowwise HIP op, with tensor checks, stride assumptions, stream semantics, and PyTorch/OneFlow integration notes.
outputs: Framework tensor Y shaped [rows] with one row sum per row
constraints: Template-only scaffold; no framework dispatch timing is claimed
Reject or explicitly handle non-GPU, non-float32, and non-contiguous inputs
Record whether timing includes Python dispatch, extension launch, synchronization, compiler warmup, or graph capture

verification.method: Compare extension output with torch.sum(x, dim=1) or equivalent OneFlow eager output across contiguous and rejected-stride cases.

curation.notes: Framework extension scaffold for rowwise HIP work; useful for comparing eager, compiler-generated, Triton, and custom HIP paths.
